issues.description: failedRuns = runs.filter(r => r.state !== 'completed') includes 'initializing' and 'unknown' state runs in the failed cohort, potentially diluting failedAvg. The existing parseOrchestratorLogs already filters out 'initializing' runs for its own summaries; applying the same filter here would make the metric more precise. Current behavior is consistent with the test suite so no functional break, but worth aligning.

The custom test() and describe() runner functions are declared after their first use (line 5), relying on JavaScript function-declaration hoisting. This is technically correct but unconventional placement. Consider moving the runner definition to the top of the file for readability.

Comment 'neutral analysis — no thresholds exceeded' is incorrect: the default makeAnalysis() data (S.rate=83, M.rate=60) triggers the m_task_splitting recommendation (23-point gap > 15-point threshold). The test behavior is correct but the comment misleads readers about what the default fixture represents.

failedRuns is defined as runs.filter(r => r.state !== 'completed'), which includes 'unknown' and 'initializing' states. This can skew failedAvg in taskCountCorrelation if the caller passes unfiltered runs. Current call sites in parseAllSessions filter out 'initializing' upstream, but the function has no guard of its own.
